title: terminalis australis
intro: Terminalis australis, commonly known as the 'Kakadu Plum', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The therapeutic actions of the Kakadu Plum are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system, protect against oxidative stress, and exhibit antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
side_effects: Terminalis australis can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive bleeding, nausea, and dizziness. In severe cases, it can cause respiratory failure, cardiac arrest, and even death. Improper use of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, hives, and anaphylaxis. If ingested in large quantities or taken without proper guidance, terminalis australis can cause long-term health issues, including liver damage and gastrointestinal problems.
